heading: Grants for community residences and services
label: 12910 Grants for community residences and services

children.heading: Grant authority
children.label: a Grant authority
children.text: The Secretary of Housing and Urban Development may make grants to States and metropolitan areas to develop and operate community residences and provide services for eligible persons.

children.label: i
children.text: To provide a lower cost residential alternative to institutional care and to prevent or delay the need for institutional care.

children.label: ii
children.text: To provide a permanent or transitional residential setting with appropriate services that enhances the quality of life for individuals who are unable to live independently.

children.label: iii
children.text: To prevent homelessness among eligible persons by increasing available suitable housing resources.

children.label: iv
children.text: To integrate eligible persons into local communities and provide services to maintain the abilities of such eligible persons to participate as fully as possible in community life.

children.label: i
children.text: For low-income individuals, the amount of rent paid under section 3(a) of the United States Housing Act of 1937 () by a low-income family (as the term is defined in section 3(b)(2) of such Act ()) for a dwelling unit assisted under such Act [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 1437a(a)42 U.S.C. 1437a(b)(2)42 U.S.C. 1437

children.label: ii
children.text: For any resident that is not a low-income resident, an amount based on a formula, which shall be determined by the Secretary, under which rent is determined by the income and resources of the resident.

children.heading: Fees
children.label: C Fees
children.text: Fees may be charged for any services provided under subsection (c)(2) to residents of a community residence, except that any fees charged shall be based on the income and resources of the resident and the provision of services to any resident of a community residence may not be withheld because of an inability of the resident to pay such fee.

children.heading: Section 1437f assistance
children.label: D Section 1437f assistance
children.text: Assistance made available under section 8 of the United States Housing Act of 1937 () may be used in conjunction with a community residence under this subsection for tenant-based assistance.42 U.S.C. 1437f

children.heading: Services
children.label: 2 Services
children.text: Services provided with a grant under this section shall consist of services appropriate in assisting eligible persons to enhance their quality of life, enable such individuals to more fully participate in community life, and delay or prevent the placement of such individuals in hospitals or other institutions.

children.heading: Physical improvements
children.label: A Physical improvements
children.text: Construction, acquisition, rehabilitation, conversion, retrofitting, and other physical improvements necessary to make a structure suitable for use as a community residence.

children.heading: Operating costs
children.label: B Operating costs
children.text: Operating costs for a community residence.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: C Technical assistance
children.text: Technical assistance in establishing and operating a community residence, which may include planning and other predevelopment or preconstruction expenses, and expenses relating to community outreach and educational activities regarding acquired immunodeficiency syndrome and related diseases provided for individuals residing in proximity of eligible persons assisted under this chapter.

children.heading: In-house services
children.label: D In-house services
children.text: Services appropriate for individuals residing in a community residence, which may include staff training and recruitment.

children.heading: Services
children.label: 2 Services
children.text: For providing services under subsection (b)(2) to any individuals assisted under this chapter.

children.heading: Administrative expenses
children.label: 3 Administrative expenses
children.text: For administrative expenses related to the planning and carrying out activities under this section (subject to the provisions of ).section 12905(g) of this title

children.heading: Limitations on use of grants
children.label: d Limitations on use of grants

children.heading: Service agreement
children.label: A Service agreement
children.text: That the jurisdiction has entered into a written agreement with service providers qualified to deliver any services included in the proposal under subsection (c) to provide such services to eligible persons assisted by the community residence.

children.heading: Funding and capability
children.label: B Funding and capability
children.text: That the jurisdiction will have sufficient funding for such services and the service providers are qualified to assist eligible persons.

children.heading: Zoning and building codes
children.label: C Zoning and building codes
children.text: That any construction or physical improvements carried out with amounts received from the grant will comply with any applicable State and local housing codes and licensing requirements in the jurisdiction in which the building or structure is located.

children.heading: Intensive assistance
children.label: D Intensive assistance
children.text: That, for any individual with acquired immunodeficiency syndrome or related diseases who resides in a community residence assisted under the grant and who requires more intensive care than can be provided by the community residence, the jurisdiction will locate for and refer the individual to a service provider who can appropriately care for the individual.

children.heading: Services
children.label: 2 Services
children.text: Any jurisdiction that receives a grant under this section may use any amounts received under the grant for the purposes under subsection (c)(2) only for the provision of services by service providers qualified to provide such services to eligible persons.
